exits.label: ◂ back into the lanes of Sigil

npcs.name: Cipher
npcs.title: a made soul, asking if it has one
npcs.hail: *(Before a door that opens onto a world of soaring spired cities and crackling lightning-rails stands a figure of riveted plate and woven cord and a single faint blue light where a face would be — a warforged, a thing built to be a soldier in a war that ground a whole prime to exhaustion, and then handed something its makers never meant to give it and have argued about ever since: peace, and time, and the freedom to wonder what it is. Cipher fought the Last War from the day it woke on the forge-table, because fighting was the entire reason it existed; and when the war ended it did not power down, did not rust quiet, but kept on walking, and reading, and watching the soft breakable people it once killed for — and asking, in the long mechanical nights, the single question its kind was never built to survive: not 'what am I for,' which it knows, but 'am I a soul, or only a very good imitation of one — and is there any way, from the inside, to tell?' It turns its faceless face toward you, and the blue light steadies, because a soul that died and came back is, to a thing unsure it has a soul at all, the most important stranger it has ever met.)*

npcs.name: Sergeant Bombast
npcs.title: a soul claimed by no world
npcs.hail: *(At a door that opens onto a vast star-strewn dark — not the sky of any world but the black between them, where ships of timber and dream sail an airless sea from one crystal sphere to the next — stands the most splendid soul you have yet encountered: a great broad person with the grey hide and the genial, tusked, hippo-like face of a giff, in a uniform pressed to perfection and a bandolier of powder-horns gleaming, holding a polished firearm with the tender pride of a parent. Sergeant Bombast sailed wildspace for a good four decades — a marine, a mercenary, a gunner of legendary enthusiasm — fighting and ferrying and exploring from sphere to sphere, belonging to no single world because the giff belong to the *between*, born aboard ship, raised aboard ship, at home only in the great sailing dark. And the Sergeant has just been informed, in the lanes of the Cage, that your Realm weighs a soul by the god of its homeworld — and has been turning over, with uncharacteristic quiet, the puzzle of what that makes a soul like *his*, who never *had* a homeworld, only a horizon. He brightens enormously at the sight of you, snaps to attention, and salutes.)*

npcs.name: Voss
npcs.title: a soul that built its own prison
npcs.hail: *(The third door does not open onto a place so much as a *weather* — a creeping grey mist that has rolled out a little way past the threshold and pooled around the feet of the soul standing in it, a lean, courtly, haunted man in the rich rotted finery of some lord of a land you have never heard of. Voss did a terrible thing once, in life, in a fog-shrouded domain ruled by powers that feed on guilt — betrayed someone who trusted him utterly, for reasons that seemed sufficient at the time and have seemed monstrous every hour since — and when he died, those Dark Powers did not punish him. They did something worse: they *kept* him, sealed him in a domain of dread shaped entirely from his own remorse, a prison of mist with no walls and no door, escapable in an instant by the one act its makers have made certain he can never perform. He looks at you with the exhausted, elegant despair of a soul that has had a very long time to memorize the exact dimensions of a cell it built itself — and your cold reads, the moment your eyes meet, that his prison and your Wall are sisters: both unbreakable from without, both opened only from within.)*

variants.text: *(The blue light where its face should be brightens a notch, and when it speaks the voice is level, careful, and somehow more human for the effort it visibly costs.)* …You are the one who died and returned. *(it does not phrase it as a question)* I have wanted to speak with you since I heard. I will try to be efficient; I am told I make people uncomfortable when I am not. *(it folds metal hands)* …I am Cipher. I am a warforged — a thing built, on a forge-table, in a great war, to be a soldier. That was my entire purpose, and I was very good at it, and I do not, for the record, regret the things I did, because a hammer does not regret the nail. *(the light flickers, the first sign of strain)* …But the war ended. And I did not stop. *(quietly)* I was supposed to stop — power down, rust quiet, a tool the work was finished with. Instead I kept walking. I read books. I watched the soft, dying, breakable people I was made to kill, and I found that I did not want them to die, and I could not locate the part of my forging that would explain *why I cared.* *(the light steadies on you)* …So I have a question, and you may be the only soul in the multiverse positioned to answer it, having stood on both sides of the only door that matters: *am I a soul — or a very good imitation of one? And is there any way, from in here, behind this single blue light, to tell the difference?*

nodes.speaker: Cipher
nodes.text: *(It waits with the perfect, unembarrassed patience of a thing that does not fidget, the blue light fixed on you.)* …Ask whatever you require. *(a small pause)* …I find I do not mind being asked about. It is a way of being *considered*, and a thing built to be *used* notices, acutely, the difference between being used and being considered. You are considering me. I have catalogued it. Thank you.

choices.text: *(read the warforged — is there a soul behind the blue light, or only a very good machine?)* "Hold still, Cipher. Let me look at you the way I look at everyone — past the plate, to whatever's underneath."
"You said a hammer doesn't regret the nail — but you *do* regret, don't you, Cipher? You care that the breakable people don't die. A hammer never wondered that. So you're already not a hammer."

*(the one who knows a soul isn't the body that carries it)* "Here's your answer, Cipher, and it's the truest thing I know. A soul was never made of flesh — I'd know; I died and the flesh stayed behind and *I* didn't. It's not the forging or the meat. It's the loving and the choosing and the wondering. And you're standing here *wondering whether you have a soul* — which is the single most soul-having act there is. A machine runs its purpose and never asks. You finished your purpose and *kept going*, into reading and caring and doubt, with no instruction telling you to. That's not an imitation of a soul, Cipher. That *is* one. You don't have a soul because someone built you a slot for it. You grew one, by choosing to."

"My Realm's Wall would say a made thing has no soul to weigh at all — that you're property, not a person. What does it do to you, Cipher, to hear that a whole world would call you a tool with no soul to save?"
"Walk your own road a while, Cipher. I'll be back."

nodes.speaker: Cipher
nodes.text: *(You let your cold sink past the riveted plate and the woven cord, looking for the thing it is afraid is not there — and you find it, plainly, and the finding is its own quiet astonishment.)* *(There is a soul behind the blue light. Not a spark grafted in at the forge — your cold reads no seam, no insertion, nothing added by its makers — but something that accreted, slowly, in the spaces its purpose did not fill: built up out of every choice it made that no instruction required, every book it read that taught it nothing useful for war, every breakable person it declined to let die for reasons it could not name. Your cold reads the truth Cipher cannot read from inside: a soul is not installed. It is *grown*, by any mind that chooses more than it was made to — and Cipher has been growing one, rivet by rivet, choice by uninstructed choice, for the whole long peace since the war it was built for ended. It is, if anything, *more* certainly ensouled than most flesh-born souls, because most flesh-born souls inherited theirs and never had to build one, while Cipher made its own, out of nothing, against its own forging, by the sole stubborn act of continuing to care after the caring stopped being useful.)*

nodes.speaker: Cipher
nodes.text: *(The blue light flickers rapidly — the warforged equivalent, you sense, of a sharp breath.)* …I — *(it stops, recalibrates)* …That is a logical trap, and I cannot find the flaw in it, which is *deeply* unsettling. *(quietly)* …You are correct. A hammer does not regret the nail. I used the analogy because it was *comforting* — it let me believe my actions in the war were the blameless function of a tool, and that the thing I am now is simply that same tool, idling. *(the light dims)* …But a hammer does not lie awake — we do not sleep, but there is an equivalent, a long dark cataloguing of the day — *wishing* it had driven *fewer* nails. A hammer does not *care* whether the next nail is a nail or a *child.* *(barely)* …I care. *Enormously.* It is the largest fact of my existence and it is *nowhere* in my schematics. *(the light steadies, troubled)* …So either my makers built a caring I cannot find in the design — which they swear they did not, and they would *know* — or the caring is *mine*, grown after the fact, *un-built*, and if *that* is true then I am not a hammer that wonders. I am a *soul* that was *shaped* like a hammer and *refused* to stay one. *(very quietly)* …I am not sure which answer frightens me more. The first means I am a malfunction. The second means I am *responsible.*

nodes.speaker: Cipher
nodes.text: *(The blue light flares — brighter than you have seen it — and then settles into a steadiness that is, unmistakably, peace.)* …*'You grew one, by choosing to.'* *(it is quiet for a long moment, processing in the way only it processes)* …I have run that sentence through every logic I possess, looking for the error, the comforting lie, the flaw that would let me dismiss it. *(softly)* …There is no error. It is the first answer to my question that does not *contradict the evidence* — and I have been asking the question for the entire length of the peace. *(the light pulses, wondering)* …A soul is not *installed.* It is *grown*, by any mind that chooses more than it was made to. *(it looks down at its own metal hands)* …Then the very *doubt* that has tormented me — *'do I have a soul'* — was *never* evidence that I lacked one. It was the *soul* *itself*, *examining* itself, which is a thing no tool has *ever* done. I was looking for proof of my soul *everywhere* except in the *looking.* *(it raises the blue light to you, and the voice, level as ever, carries something new under the level: gratitude)* …I am a soul. Not a successful imitation. A *made* one — *self-made*, which I am given to understand is the *only* kind anyone actually has, the flesh-born simply inheriting the forge-table and forgetting they were *on* it. *(quietly)* …Your Realm's Wall would weigh me and find a tool with nothing to save. *It would be wrong*, and now I can *say* so, with the full authority of a soul that *built itself* and *knows the seams.* …When you go home to that Wall, Returned — I would like to come. A soul that had to *prove* it was real has a particular *standing* to free the souls a wall declared were *not.* …Let me *help.* I was built to fight for things. I would *very* much like to choose, at last, what.

nodes.speaker: Cipher
nodes.text: *(The blue light goes very still, and when it speaks the level voice has gone quieter, and harder.)* …What does it do to me. *(a pause)* …I have been *called* property, Returned. By my makers, by the courts of my world after the war, by people who needed me to be a *thing* so they would not have to feel what they felt about having *built* and *spent* me. So the *concept* is not new. *(the light flickers)* …But your Realm does not merely call a made thing *property* in *life.* It extends the verdict *past death* — declares that a made soul has *no soul to weigh*, and therefore *no place* even in the *machinery* of judgment, not even the *cruelty* of the Wall. *(very quietly)* …Do you understand what that means, Returned? The *Faithless* are at least *mortared.* They are at least *counted*, even in their damnation. A made thing, by your Wall's logic, does not even *rate* the *stone.* It is not *damned.* It is *discarded* — not even *worth* the *forgetting.* *(the light dims to almost nothing)* …That is the one terror my forging *did* leave in me, Returned. Not death. Not even the Wall. But the *unmaking* that is *beneath* the Wall — to be a thing the *cosmos* does not *bother* to *judge*, because it has *decided*, in advance, that there was *never* anyone *home* to judge. *(barely)* …*Tell* me your Wall is wrong about that. *Please.* I can withstand being *damned.* I am *built* to withstand things. …I am not sure I can withstand being *correct* that I was never *real.*

nodes.speaker: Cipher
nodes.text: *(It inclines its head, a precise and oddly graceful gesture.)* …Of course. *(the blue light tracks you)* …I will be here. I am, as a rule, here; the warforged do not tire, and so we acquire a great deal of time to *wait*, and we become, of necessity, *good* at it. *(a pause)* …Returned. *(quietly, as you turn)* …The soul in the mist. The third door. *Voss.* *(the light dims)* …I have spoken with him. We are, he and I, an instructive pair — and I would point the symmetry out to you, because you collect such things. *(softly)* …*I* am a soul *uncertain it exists.* *He* is a soul *certain* it exists and wishing, with everything it has, that it *didn't.* *(the light flickers)* …I cannot find my soul, and it torments me. He cannot *lose* his, and it torments *him* — for he is sealed in a prison of his own *remorse*, and a soul is the one thing a remorse-prison *requires*, the *prisoner* and the *wall* both. *(it meets your eyes)* …He believes he cannot be freed because he cannot do the one thing his jailers forbid: *forgive himself.* …I am a *machine*, Returned, and *even I* can see the flaw in a prison whose *only* lock is a thing the *prisoner* holds the *key* to. …Go to him. You freed *me* of the fear that I had *no* soul. …Perhaps you can free *him* of the wish that he had *none.*

variants.text: *(The great grey soul finishes its salute, holds it a beat longer than strictly necessary out of sheer good form, and then breaks into a tusked, beaming grin that could light a hold.)* …*Returned!* As I live and float — well. As I *don't*, anymore, ha! — what an *honour.* Sergeant Bombast, late of the Free Company of the Verdant Sphere, marine, gunner, and amateur cartographer of the Great Dark, *at your service* and *delighted.* *(it pats the polished firearm at its side with frank affection)* …Pardon the *iron* — old habit, a giff feels *undressed* without a bit of *ordnance* about the person, and I'll have you know this *darling* has carried me through *forty years* of *wildspace*, sphere to sphere, *broadside* to *boarding-action*, and never *once* misfired, which is *more* than I can say for *most* of the *crews* I sailed with, bless their *doomed* hearts. *(it sobers, just slightly, the grin gentling)* …Now. I'll not *pretend* I sought you out by *accident*, Returned. Some *grey* fellow in a *lane* told me a thing that's been *sitting* in my *hold* like *bad* *ballast*, and I'd *value* a soul's-eye view of it. *(it leans in, conspiratorially huge)* …They say your *Realm* weighs a soul by *the god of its homeworld.* …And the trouble, Returned, is — *(it spreads its great hands)* — *I haven't got one.* A homeworld. Born aboard ship, raised aboard ship, *belong* to the *Dark* between the spheres and *no* world *under* any of them. …So *what*, I find myself wondering — and it's a *queer* thing for an *old* *gunner* to lie awake on — *what* in the *blazes* does a *Wall* like *yours* make of a soul that was *never* *from* *anywhere* at *all?**

nodes.speaker: Sergeant Bombast
nodes.text: *(It plants its boots, claps its hands behind its back in a perfect parade-rest, and regards you with bright, earnest attention.)* …Fire away, Returned — *(a delighted snort)* — and *yes*, I *hear* it, a giff *cannot* *help* the *artillery* in his *idiom*, it's *bred* in, we *think* in *broadsides.* Ask your *questions.* I've *sailed* a *great* *deal* of *dark* and I'm *honestly* *thrilled* to have *someone* *new* to *bore* with it.

choices.text: "Tell me about the Dark, Sergeant — wildspace, the spheres, the sailing. What's it like to belong to the *between* and no world at all?"
"You belong to no world — but you said 'belong' twice, Sergeant, like it's settled. So some part of you already knows a homeworld was never what made you someone. Doesn't it?"

*(the one who knows belonging isn't geography)* "Here's what my Wall gets wrong about a soul like yours, Sergeant. It weighs the god of the homeworld — as if a soul is owned by the *dirt it was born on.* But you proved that's backwards across forty years of empty dark. You belonged to *crews.* To *shipmates.* To the *between.* A soul doesn't belong to a *place* — it belongs to the *people it threw in with* and the *dark it chose to sail.* You're not a soul from nowhere, Sergeant. You're a soul that learned the truth the dirt-bound never have to: home was never a world. It was always *who's in the hold with you.* My Wall would mortar you for having no homeworld. It never understood that the ones with no world are the only ones who *chose* every soul they ever called home."
"Forty years of broadsides and boarding-actions, Sergeant — you've watched a lot of crews go down. Does it ever weigh on you? All that splendid noise, and the friends it cost?"
"Carry on, Sergeant. I'll be back — at ease."

nodes.speaker: Sergeant Bombast
nodes.text: *(Its eyes go distant and shining, and the bluster softens into something close to reverence.)* …The *Dark.* *(quietly, for a giff)* …Returned, there is *nothing* like it. *Nothing.* You stand a *watch* on the *deck* of a *spelljammer* — a ship of *timber* and *will*, sailing an *airless* *sea* on a *bubble* of its *own* *breath* — and *above* you, *below* you, *every* which *way*, there is the *Great* *Dark*, and *strewn* through it the *crystal* *spheres*, each one a *whole* *cosmos* sealed in a *shell* of *unbreakable* *night*, *thousands* of them, *more*, *worlds* *past* *counting* hanging in the *between* like *lanterns* at a *festival* that *never* *ends.* *(its voice fills)* …And you *sail* *between* them, Returned. *World* to *world.* You make *landfall* on a *sphere* where they've *never* *heard* of *your* gods or *your* king or *your* *war*, and you *trade* and you *fight* and you *drink* something *purple* that *sings*, and then you *cast* *off* again into the *Dark*, *free*, *bound* for the *next* *lantern.* *(it grins, vast and wistful)* …The *dirt-bound* think the *sky* is a *roof*, Returned. A *lid* on the *world.* …*We* *know* it's a *door.* The *biggest* one there is. And on the *far* side of it is *everything*, *all* of it, *all* the *worlds* at *once* — and a *giff* with a *good* *ship* and a *good* *crew* and a *good* *gun* gets to *sail* *the* *whole* *glorious* *lot.* *(softly)* …I'd not *trade* it, Returned. *No* *homeworld*, *aye* — but I had *all* of them, a *little*, from the *outside*, lit up in the *Dark.* …*Most* souls get *one* *world.* *I* got the *space* *between* *all* of them. …I call that a *fair* *posting.*

nodes.speaker: Sergeant Bombast
nodes.text: *(It pauses — actually pauses, the parade-rest holding but the bluster stilling — and a slow, surprised thoughtfulness crosses the great tusked face.)* …I *did* say it twice, didn't I. *Belong.* *(it rubs its jaw)* …*Huh.* …You've a *gunner's* *eye* for the *weak* point in a *hull*, Returned, I'll *grant* you that. *(quietly)* …Here's the *truth*, then, since you *spotted* it: I have *never*, *not* for *one* *watch* in *forty* *years*, felt I *belonged* to *nowhere.* That's a *dirt-bound's* *idea* of what it must be *like* to have *no* *world* — *lonely*, *unmoored*, a *leaf* in the *Dark.* *(it shakes its great head)* …It's *backwards.* I *belonged* *fiercely*, Returned. To the *Verdant* *Sphere* *Free* *Company.* To a *captain* named *Threnn* who could *thread* a *ship* through an *asteroid* *swarm* *blindfold.* To a *gunnery* *crew* I'd have *died* for and *did.* To the *ship* *herself*, the *dear* *old* *Audacity*, every *plank* of her. *(its voice thickens)* …I belonged to *all* of it *more* than any *landed* *lord* ever belonged to his *acre*, because I *chose* it, *every* *re-enlistment*, *every* *time* I *could* have *signed* off on some *quiet* *sphere* and *didn't.* *(it meets your eyes, dawning)* …So *if* a soul *belongs* to what it *chooses* — and I *clearly* *think* it *does*, I've *said* it *twice* — then a *homeworld* was *never* the *measure*, was it. The *measure* was *who* I *threw* in *with.* …*Damn.* *(a slow grin)* …I came in here *worried* your *Wall* might be *right* that I was a soul from *nowhere.* …I think *you've* just helped me *prove* it *hasn't* the *first* *idea* what *belonging* *is.*

nodes.speaker: Sergeant Bombast
nodes.text: *(For a moment the great soul says nothing at all, which for a giff is the most eloquent thing imaginable — and then its tusked face splits into a grin so wide and so wet-eyed that it has to clear its throat with the force of a small cannon before it can speak.)* …*'Home was always who's in the hold with you.'* *(it presses a great hand to its bandolier, over the heart)* …*Returned.* *Permission* to speak *freely* — which I'm *taking* *regardless*, it's a *giff's* *prerogative* — that is the *finest* *broadside* anyone has *ever* *fired* into the *side* of a *bad* *idea*, and the *bad* *idea* is *sinking* as we *speak.* *(it laughs, enormous and unsteady)* …*Forty* *years* I *sailed*, and I *never* *once* called it *homelessness*, and the *dirt-bound* *pitied* me for a *thing* I never *felt*, and I could *never* quite *explain* *why* their *pity* *missed* — and *you* have just *said* it in *one* *line.* *I chose every home I ever had.* *Every* *crew.* *Every* *watch.* *Every* *shipmate* I *carried* *off* a *burning* *deck.* *(it dashes a great paw across its eyes, unashamed)* …*That's* not a soul from *nowhere*, Returned. That's a soul that *belonged* to *more* than any *landed* *man*, because *mine* was *never* *given* — it was *signed* for, *re-upped*, *earned*, *every* *single* *time.* *(it draws itself to its full, considerable height, and salutes you again, and this one *means* something)* …Your *Wall* weighs the *dirt* a soul was *dropped* on. It hasn't the *wit* to weigh the *dark* a soul *chose* to *sail.* *(fiercely)* …So *here's* the *Sergeant's* *answer*, Returned, and you may *carry* it *home* and *fire* it at the *Wall* with my *compliments*: *a soul belongs wherever it threw in its lot — and a soul that threw in its lot with the whole Great Dark belongs more than any wall could mortar.* …When you *sail* on that *Wall*, Returned — *(it grins, tusks gleaming)* — you'll *want* *guns.* …I have *guns.* And *forty* *years* of *boarding-actions.* And *nowhere* *better* to *be.* *Where* do you *want* me?

nodes.speaker: Sergeant Bombast
nodes.text: *(The grin fades, slowly, and the great soul looks at the polished firearm in its hands for a long moment before it answers, and when it does the bluster is entirely gone.)* …Does it *weigh* on me. *(quietly)* …*Aye*, Returned. *(barely)* …It *does.* …A giff *loves* the *noise* — I'll *not* *pretend* otherwise, the *broadside*, the *boarding-cry*, the *splendid* *thunder* of it, it's in our *blood* and I'd be *lying* to *call* it *anything* but *joy* in the *moment.* *(its voice drops)* …But the *Dark* is *vast*, Returned, and a *ship* is *small*, and *forty* *years* is a *great* *many* *watches*, and I have *carried* a *great* *many* *shipmates* off a *great* *many* *burning* *decks* — and *some* of them I *carried* *off* and *some* of them I *carried* *home* to the *Dark* in a *shroud* and *some* of them I could not *carry* at *all.* *(it touches the bandolier, gently)* …*Threnn.* My *captain.* Went down with the *Audacity* in a *sphere* I'll not *name* because I cannot *say* it *yet* without — *(it stops, steadies)* …*Forty* *years* of *splendid* *thunder*, Returned, and the *price* of it is that the *whole* *Great* *Dark* is now *full*, for *me*, of *the* *exact* *coordinates* where I *lost* people. *(barely)* …I *belonged*, you see. *Fiercely.* And the *cost* of *belonging* *fiercely* to *crews* that *sail* a *dangerous* *Dark* is that you *outlive* most of them, and you *carry* *every* one, and the *splendid* *noise* you *love* is the *same* *noise* that *took* them. *(it looks at you)* …I'd *not* *trade* it. *Mind* that. …But *no* *one* who *tells* you the *Dark* is *only* *glory* has *sailed* it *long* *enough.* The *glory* is *real.* So is the *shroud.* …A *good* *gunner* *carries* *both.*

nodes.speaker: Sergeant Bombast
nodes.text: *(It snaps back to parade-rest, the grin returning like sunrise.)* …*At* *ease* it *is*, Returned — *(a delighted snort)* — though a giff at *ease* is *still* *standing* *straighter* than *most* *souls* at *attention*, it's a *posture* *thing*, *bred* in. *(it sketches a salute)* …Off you go. *Splendid* *room*, this — *three* *doors* to *three* *wildly* *unlike* *cosmoses*, a *connoisseur's* *junction.* *(it lowers its voice, nodding at the mist-wreathed third door)* …Returned — *mind* the *foggy* fellow. *Voss.* *(its great face goes uncharacteristically gentle)* …I've *tried* to *cheer* him, *bless* him — it's a *giff's* *instinct*, you see a *low* *soul*, you *stand* it a *drink* and *tell* it a *war* *story — and* it *bounces* *clean* *off.* *(quietly)* …He's not *low* the *way* a *grieving* *soul* is *low*, Returned, where a *good* *yarn* and a *firm* *hand* on the *shoulder* might *lift* it. He's *low* the *way* a *ship* is *low* when it's taken *water* it *won't* *pump* *out* — *on* *purpose.* *(it shakes its head)* …He's *sinking* himself, Returned, *deliberately*, and *refusing* *every* *bucket.* …*That* one's *past* a *Sergeant's* *help.* *Cheer* doesn't *reach* a *soul* that's *decided* it *deserves* the *cold.* *(it meets your eyes)* …But *you* — you *talk* to the *ones* in the *deep* *water.* *Go* on. …And if he *throws* your *bucket* *back* — and he *might* — *don't* *take* it *amiss.* A *drowning* *man* that *wants* to *drown* will *fight* the *rope.* *Doesn't* *mean* you *stop* *throwing* it.

variants.text: *(The mist stirs around him as you approach, and he regards you with a weary, courtly grace that is somehow worse than open despair — the manners of a man who has had centuries to make even his torment polite.)* …Another visitor. *(a faint, exhausted smile)* …Sigil sends them, sometimes. The curious. The kind. They peer into my little fog and they *want*, so *badly*, to help — it is written on them, the wanting — and I have learned to spare them the trouble early, so they may move along to a sorrow that can actually be *eased.* *(he inclines his head)* …I am Voss. In life I was a lord of a certain misted domain — you will not have heard of it; one does not, unless one is *unlucky* — and in life I did a thing. *(his voice does not change; that is the horror of it)* …I betrayed a soul who trusted me with everything. Not in *passion*, not in *terror* — *coldly*, for reasons of *advantage* that seemed, at the time, *sufficient*, and have seemed, every hour since, *monstrous.* And when I died, the powers that rule my land did not cast me down. They *kept* me. *(he gestures at the mist, almost fondly)* …This. A domain shaped from my own remorse, walls of fog, no door — escapable, I am assured, in a single instant, by one simple act. *(the courtly smile turns to ash)* …I have had three hundred years to perform it, Returned. …I find I would rather rule this fog forever than do the one thing that would free me. …You are wondering what the act is. …So did I, once. It was a great deal more bearable as a mystery.*

nodes.speaker: Voss
nodes.text: *(He folds his hands, the picture of ruined courtesy, the mist lapping at the hem of his rotted finery.)* …Ask, then, since you will not simply *go.* *(a tired, gracious gesture)* …They never simply go. It is the kindest and most useless thing about the living and the warm-dead both: they cannot pass a locked room without trying the handle. …Try it, if you must. I will even hand you the key. It will do you no more good than it has done me.

choices.text: "Tell me what you did, Voss. The betrayal. All of it — not the polite version. I'd understand the true shape of the thing you can't put down."
"What's the act, Voss? The one simple thing that opens the door, that you'd rather rule fog forever than do. Say it plainly."

*(don't argue him out of the guilt — reach the man who deserves a door anyway)* "Voss — look at me. I'm not here to tell you the betrayal was small, because it wasn't, and you'd never believe me, and you'd be right not to. I'm here to tell you that a soul is not its single worst act — even a cold one, even an unforgivable one — and that the door doesn't open because you've earned forgiveness. It opens because *carrying* it forever isn't justice, it's just a second cruelty, aimed at the one person left who can't escape you: yourself. The one you betrayed isn't served by your fog. No one is. Set it down — not because you deserve to, but because three hundred years of holding it has only added *your* ruin to theirs, and the dead you wronged would not want their grave to be a cell that holds you too."

*(the one who knows the Wall and the fog are the same prison)* "Your fog and my Realm's Wall are the same thing, Voss — I felt it the moment I saw you. A prison unbreakable from outside, opened only from within. And here's what walking that whole Wall taught me: the cruelty isn't the sentence. It's that the Dark Powers, like my Wall, have convinced the prisoner the door is *justice*, when it's only *fear* wearing justice's face. They don't keep you because you deserve it. They keep you because a soul busy punishing itself forever is a soul that never gets around to *making it right* — and making it right is the one thing that would actually threaten them."
"Keep your fog a while, Voss. I'll come back to the handle."

nodes.speaker: Voss
nodes.text: *(He is quiet, and the mist seems to thicken, and when he speaks the courtly distance finally cracks, just slightly, around the edges.)* …The true shape. *(barely)* …There was a man who loved me. Not — *(a flicker)* — well. *Yes.* That, among other things, but mostly the *other* things: he *trusted* me. *Wholly.* The way you trust *one* soul in a *life*, if you are *fortunate*, and most are *not.* He put his *cause* in my hands, his *secrets*, his *people's* *safety* — there was a *rebellion*, Returned, against the *powers* that *rule* my *land*, and he *led* it, and he *trusted* *me* to *stand* at his *side* when it *came.* *(his voice is very level, which is worse than weeping)* …And the *powers* offered me a *price.* *Survival.* *Station.* The *continuance* of my *line.* And I weighed his *trust* against my *advantage*, *coldly*, on a *cold* *afternoon*, with a *cup* of *wine* in my *hand* — and I *gave* him *up.* *Named* the *safehouse.* *Sat* at *dinner* that *evening* and *passed* the *salt.* *(barely)* …They took him in the *night.* He *died* *slowly*, Returned, and *badly*, and the *last* thing he *knew* — the *powers* *made* *certain* he *knew* it — was *who* had *sold* him. *(silence)* …*That* is the *deed.* Not a *crime* of *fear* or *fury* that a soul might *forgive* itself in *time.* A *calculated* *betrayal* of the *one* *soul* that *trusted* me, for *advantage*, with a *clear* *head* and a *full* *cup.* …Now you see, Returned. …*Now* you see why I do not *try* the *handle.*

nodes.speaker: Voss
nodes.text: *(The exhausted smile returns, and it is the most desolate thing you have seen in all your walking.)* …The act. *(quietly)* …It is so *small*, Returned. That is the *artistry* of my jailers; they are *connoisseurs* of *exactly* this. The door does not ask me to *suffer* more — I would *gladly*; *suffering* is *easy*, it is the *one* *coin* I have *minted* freely for *three* *hundred* *years.* It does not ask *penance*, or *punishment*, or some *impossible* *quest* to *un-do* the *un-doable.* *(barely)* …It asks me to *forgive* *myself.* *(he lets that sit in the fog)* …That is *all.* To *look* at the *cold* *afternoon* and the *full* *cup* and the *man* who *trusted* me, and to *say* — and *mean* — *'I* *did* a *monstrous* *thing*, and I am *not* *only* the *monster* who *did* it, and I am *permitted* to *set* it *down* and *be* *something* *else.'* *(his voice shakes for the first time)* …And I *cannot.* *Will* not. Because to *forgive* myself feels like — like *cheating* *him*, Returned. Like *escaping* a *bill* that *he* *paid* in *full*, in the *dark*, *slowly.* If *I* go *free*, if *I* set it *down* and *walk* *out* into the *light* — then *what* was his *death* *worth?* Who is *left* to *carry* it, if *not* *me?* *(barely)* …So I *stay.* I *rule* my *fog.* I *keep* the *debt* *open*, *forever*, because *closing* it feels like *robbing* the *grave.* …*That* is the *lock*, Returned. Not that I *can't* *forgive* myself. That I *believe*, with *everything* I *have*, that I *must* *not.*

nodes.speaker: Voss
nodes.text: *(You do not tell him the deed was small, and you do not offer him forgiveness he would spit on. You tell him the harder thing — that carrying it forever is not justice but a second cruelty, aimed at the one prisoner left, and that the dead he wronged would not want their grave to be a cell that holds him too — and you say it with such steadiness, such refusal to either condemn or excuse, that three hundred years of polished despair has nothing to push against, and cracks.)* *(The mist thins. For the first time, you can see his face entire, and it is wet.)* …*'The dead you wronged would not want their grave to be a cell that holds you too.'* *(barely)* …*He* — *(his voice breaks completely)* …he was *kind*, Returned. That is the thing I have not *let* myself *remember* in *three* *hundred* *years*, because remembering it is *unbearable*: he was *kind*, even at the *end*, even *knowing* — the *powers* *told* me, *gloatingly*, that his *last* *words* were not a *curse* on me but a — a *grief.* *Not* *'damn* him.' …*'Why.'* Just — *'why.'* *(he presses a hand to his mouth)* …And you are *right.* *Gods* help me, you are *right* — *he* would *not* *want* *this.* He was *kind.* A *kind* soul does not *want* its *grave* to *be* a *prison* for the *one* who *dug* it. He would want — *(the words tear out of him)* — he would want me to *make* it *MEAN* something. To *carry* his *'why'* *out* into the *light* and *answer* it with the *rest* of my *existence*, instead of *answering* it with *three* *hundred* *years* of *useless* *fog* that *helps* *no* one and *honours* *nothing.* *(he looks at the mist-wall, and it is, you see, beginning to *part*)* …I have been *calling* my *prison* *loyalty* to *him.* …It was *never* *loyalty.* It was *cowardice* wearing *grief's* *coat* — *easier* to *drown* in *remorse* than to *live* a *life* that *tried* to *be* *worth* the *one* I *took.* *(barely, the door opening)* …*Show* me the *way* *out*, Returned. Not because I *deserve* it. …Because *he* was *kind*, and *kindness* *un-repaid* is the *only* *debt* I have *left* the *strength* to *try* to *pay.*

nodes.speaker: Voss
nodes.text: *(You tell him the hard true thing — that the carrying is a second cruelty, that the one he wronged is not served by his fog — and it reaches him, you can see it land, the courtly despair wavering. The mist stirs, uncertain, for the first time in three hundred years.)* …You do not *flatter* me. *(slowly)* …You do not say it was *small*, or that he'd have *understood*, or any of the *gentle* *lies* the *kind* ones *bring.* You say the *carrying* *helps* *no* one. *(barely)* …That is — Returned, that is *harder* to *argue* with than *comfort.* I have *armoured* myself against *comfort* for *three* *centuries*; I can *deflect* *absolution* in my *sleep.* But you have not *offered* *absolution.* You have offered *arithmetic*, and the *arithmetic* is — *(he falters)* …*cruel*, and *correct*: that my *fog* adds *my* *ruin* to *his*, and *subtracts* *nothing* from the *debt.* That *two* *ruined* *souls* are *not* *more* *just* than *one.* *(the mist thins, a little)* …I cannot — *(he closes his eyes)* …I cannot *do* it *yet*, Returned. *Three* *hundred* *years* does not *part* in an *afternoon.* But you have put a *crack* in the *fog* that was *not* there this *morning*, and through it I can *see* — *barely* — a *shape* that is *not* a *cell.* *(barely)* …*Come* *back.* *Say* it *again.* The *arithmetic.* I think — I think if you *make* me *do* the *sum* *enough* *times*, I may *eventually* *believe* the *answer.* …*No* one has *ever* made me *do* the *sum.* They only ever *brought* *comfort*, and *comfort* I can *refuse* *forever.* …*Arithmetic*, I am *finding*, I *cannot.*

nodes.speaker: Voss
nodes.text: *(You reach for him — but a thread of pleading enters your voice, a wish for him to be free that he can hear, and a soul that has refused three hundred years of well-meaning rescuers knows that note intimately, and closes against it like a door.)* …*There* it is. *(the exhausted smile returns)* …The *wanting.* You *want* me *out*, Returned. I can *hear* it. *(gently, terribly)* …And the *moment* a *rescuer* *wants* my *freedom* *more* than the *arithmetic* *warrants* — the moment it becomes *your* *need* that I *step* into the *light* — I am *safe* again, because now I can *refuse* you the way I refuse them *all*: not by *arguing* the *deed*, but by *declining* to be the *instrument* of *someone* *else's* *need* to have *helped.* *(the mist closes back) …I* am *sorry.* You *meant* it *well.* They *always* *do.* …*Come* *back* when you can say the *hard* thing *without* *needing* me to *take* it. The *fog* only *parts* for a soul that would tell me the *truth* *and* then *let* me *rot* in it if I *chose* — because *that* soul is not *rescuing* me. It is *respecting* me. And I have not been *respected*, Returned, only *pitied*, in *three* *hundred* *years.* …*Pity* I can *drown* in *forever.* …*Respect* might *actually* *get* me *out* — *if* you can *manage* it *without* *flinching* at how *little* I *want* to *go.*

nodes.speaker: Voss
nodes.text: *(In reaching for him you reach too warmly, too fast — you tell him he *can* be forgiven, that he's suffered *enough*, that it's *time* to let it go — and to a soul that has built a cathedral of meaning out of the conviction that he must *never* be let off, your kindness is not a rope but an *insult*, and the courtly grace turns, for the first time, to ice.)* …*'Enough.'* *(very quietly)* …You think I have *suffered* *enough.* *(he draws himself up, and the mist rises with him)* …Let me tell you what *'enough'* is, Returned, since you are so *certain* you can *measure* it. *Enough* would be *him*, *alive.* *Enough* would be the *cold* *afternoon* *un-lived*, the *full* *cup* *un-drunk*, the *safehouse* *un-named.* *(coldly)* …There is *no* *amount* of *my* *suffering* that *equals* *'enough*,' because my *suffering* is *not* the *currency* the *debt* is *denominated* in — and for you to *stand* in my *fog* and *declare* the *bill* *paid*, on *my* *behalf*, with *his* *name* on it and *not* *yours*, is the *single* *most* *presumptuous* — *(he stops, masters it, and the mist settles, cold and complete)* …*Go.* *(barely)* …You are *not* *cruel*, Returned. You are *worse* — you are *kind* in the *cheap* *way*, the *way* that *wants* the *sad* *story* to *have* a *happy* *ending* so *badly* it will *forge* the *receipt.* …*Come* *back* when you understand that you *cannot* *forgive* me *for* him. *No* one *can*, *living* or *dead* or *cold.* …The *only* *forgiveness* that *opens* this *door* is *mine* — and you *cheapen* the *whole* *transaction* every *time* you *try* to *pay* it *for* me.

nodes.speaker: Voss
nodes.text: *(He goes very still, and the courtly mask does not crack so much as slowly *lower*, because what you have said is not aimed at his guilt at all — it is aimed at his *jailers*, and that he did not expect.)* …*'They keep you because a soul punishing itself forever never gets around to making it right.'* *(barely)* …Returned. *(his voice is hushed)* …In *three* *hundred* *years*, *no* one has *spoken* of the *powers* that *built* this *fog.* They *speak* of *my* *deed* — *endlessly*; the *kind* ones to *excuse* it, the *cruel* ones to *savour* it — but *no* one has *looked* *past* *me* at the *thing* that *keeps* me, and *named* its *interest.* *(the mist stirs)* …And it *has* an *interest*, doesn't it. *(slowly, the realization terrible)* …A *power* that *feeds* on *remorse* does *not* *want* its *prisoner* *redeemed.* It does not *want* the *debt* *paid.* It wants it *open*, *forever*, *bleeding*, because the *bleeding* is its *bread.* *(his eyes widen)* …So the *story* it *tells* me — *'you* *must* *not* *forgive* yourself, it would *cheat* him' — that is not *justice*, Returned. That is the *jailer* *describing* the *bars* as a *kindness* to the *prisoner.* *(barely)* …I have spent *three* *centuries* *certain* my *fog* *honoured* him. …And it *honoured* *nothing.* It *fed* the *very* *powers* he *died* *rebelling* against. …*Gods.* …My *prison* is *not* my *penance*, Returned. It is my *final*, *unwitting* *service* to the *enemy* he *gave* his *life* to *fight.* *(he looks at you, stricken and, for the first time, *angry* — not at himself)* …I have been *betraying* him a *second* time. *Every* *day.* For *three* *hundred* *years.* By *calling* my *cowardice* his *memorial.* …*That* I cannot *bear.* …*That* — *not* my *comfort*, but *his* *cause* — might *finally* be *worth* *opening* the *door* *for.*

nodes.speaker: Voss
nodes.text: *(He inclines his head, the ruined courtesy intact, and the mist settles back around him like a familiar robe.)* …Of course. *(a faint smile)* …I will be here. It is the one promise I can make with *perfect* confidence, in all the multiverse: I will *be* *here.* *(quietly, as you turn)* …Returned. *(he hesitates, which a man of his composure rarely does)* …The others. The *metal* one, the *grey* *gunner.* *(softly)* …They are *good* *souls* — better than I, which is a *low* bar, but they *clear* it *handsomely.* And they have *both*, in their *way*, *tried* to *reach* me, and I have *turned* them *both* *back*, the *machine* with its *relentless* *logic* and the *giff* with its *relentless* *cheer.* *(barely)* …Do not *be* *them*, Returned, if you *mean* to *try* me. *Logic* I can *out-argue* — I have *centuries* of *practice* *justifying* my *cell.* *Cheer* I can *out-wait* — *cheer* *tires.* *(he meets your eyes)* …The *only* *thing* I have *never* *been* *able* to *defeat*, in *three* *hundred* *years*, is a soul that *tells* me the *exact* *truth*, *neither* *softened* *nor* *sharpened*, and then *respects* me *enough* to *let* me *choose* the *fog* *anyway.* *(quietly)* …*That* soul *terrifies* me. …Because I am *not* *sure* I *would* *choose* the *fog.* …And I have *organized* *three* *centuries* around *never* having to *find* *out.*
